expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs edit command with --description flag
expectations.evidence: Uses raw curl commands instead of jira.mjs. States 'Without a dedicated Atlassian skill or helper scripts, there is no automated way for the agent to interact with the Jira API.'
Manually constructs ADF JSON with heading node instead of using markdown ## syntax. The ADF is hand-crafted, not from markdown input.
Uses ADF marks (strong, em) directly in JSON rather than markdown **bold** and *italic* syntax.

expectations.text: Description contains a bullet list (- item syntax)
expectations.evidence: Constructs ADF bulletList JSON nodes manually rather than using markdown - item syntax.

expectations.text: Description contains a markdown table (| col | syntax)
expectations.evidence: Constructs ADF table JSON nodes manually rather than using markdown | col | syntax.

expectations.text: Description contains a fenced code block (```js syntax)
expectations.evidence: Constructs ADF codeBlock JSON nodes manually rather than using fenced ```js code blocks.

expectations.text: marklassian converts the markdown to valid ADF — not raw markdown text rendered in Jira
expectations.evidence: States 'No Markdown-to-ADF library or helper script' and 'Must manually construct ~200 lines of nested ADF JSON by hand'. marklassian is not used.

expectations.text: Literal \n from shell arguments are converted to real newlines before ADF conversion
expectations.evidence: No mention of \n conversion. ADF is constructed manually, so this step is irrelevant in the without-skill approach.
